source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 8. ADD(HAD1417MCW) H&abyz 인증관련 자료  20220203 (CE Dimdi)/4. TCF/10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-1 Clinical Evaluation Plan/Clinical Evaluation Plan (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-063, rev.1).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: authorized competent authorities and notified bodies
standards: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical Evaluation Plan A-TCF-5-063 Rev.1 describes the planned clinical evaluation approach for H&abyz ADD Abyz Digital Detector under MDD and MEDDEV guidance.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of the manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
keyPoints: ADD is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel X-ray detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The plan uses MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 and includes literature review, equivalent-device clinical data, PMS data, risk management, and assessment of Essential Requirements 1, 3, and 6.
Contraindications include not using the device for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
The document states sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China as TBD.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation lifecycle process
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: ADD intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: ADD contraindications
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.name: MDD Class IIa classification
concepts.evidence: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: Equivalence demonstration will be asserted in terms of three characteristics (clinical, technical, and biological).

concepts.name: PMS and PMCF activities
concepts.evidence: Through continuous PMS activity, the influence of the clinical performance and safety related to the device is constantly grasped in various aspects. In addition, PMCF studies will be conducted in accordance with the PMCF Plan if necessary.

concepts.name: Essential Requirements assessment
concepts.evidence: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements; Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements; Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements.

evidence.claim: The document is Clinical Evaluation Plan A-TCF-5-063 Rev.1 for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-063 (Rev.1)

evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 1 Update the target population group, contraindications 2020.12.10

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co.,Ltd. is the manufacturer of the ADD device.
ADD is a Class IIa device by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target user group includes radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: Target User Group A ... Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: The device is not disposable, not invasive, and not implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: Disposable Device N/A It is not disposable device; Invasive Device N/A It is not invasive device; Implantable Device N/A It is not implantable device

evidence.claim: The device uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation is planned in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 and is part of risk management according to EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4. Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012.

evidence.claim: The literature search was planned using PUBMED and Science Direct for the date range 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” are going to utilize to search for literature ... Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal metadata is general, and the document references competent authorities and notified bodies without naming a specific authority or notified body.
Sales approval status and sales volume fields for several jurisdictions are marked TBD.
The date of next clinical evaluation section is present but contains blank fields.

Some sections are written prospectively as a plan using phrases such as 'we are going to', so conclusions should not be treated as completed clinical evaluation results unless corroborated by a final report.
The source text contains minor OCR or formatting artifacts, including inconsistent names such as Jungman Lee and Jung Man Lee, and typographical errors in technical narrative.
